examples.title: Basic Mount Command Examples
examples.description: Common mount commands for different scenarios in Linux
examples.code:
mount -t ext4 /dev/sda2 /mnt/linux
mount -t vfat /dev/sdb1 /mnt/usb
mount -t ntfs-3g /dev/sdc1 /mnt/windows
mount -o ro,noexec /dev/sda3 /mnt/readonly
mount -t iso9660 /dev/cdrom /mnt/cdrom
umount /mnt/linux
examples.explanation: The -t flag specifies file system type. The -o flag sets options. Common types: ext4 (Linux), vfat (USB), ntfs-3g (Windows), iso9660 (CD/DVD). Umount detaches the file system.

examples.title: /etc/fstab Entry Format
examples.description: Configuration for automatic mounting at boot
examples.code:
# <device> <mount point> <type> <options> <dump> <fsck>
UUID=12345-6789 /boot ext4 defaults 0 2
UUID=98765-4321 / ext4 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
UUID=abcd-efgh swap swap defaults 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /mnt/data ext4 nosuid,noexec,nodev 0 2
//server/share /mnt/nfs cifs credentials=/root/pass 0 0
examples.explanation: Fields: (1) Device can be UUID/LABEL or /dev path, (2) Mount point, (3) FS type, (4) Options comma-separated, (5) Dump backup flag (0=no, 1=yes), (6) Fsck order (0=skip, 1=root, 2=other). UUIDs recommended for reliability.

examples.title: VFS Mount Operation Algorithm
examples.description: Simplified pseudocode showing how VFS handles mount request
examples.code:
PROCEDURE do_mount(device, mount_point, fs_type, flags)
 
 // Step 1: Validate inputs
 IF NOT is_valid_device(device) THEN
 RETURN ERROR_INVALID_DEVICE
 IF NOT is_empty_directory(mount_point) AND has_files(mount_point) THEN
 RETURN ERROR_DIRECTORY_NOT_EMPTY
 
 // Step 2: Get file system driver
 fs_driver = get_fs_driver(fs_type)
 IF fs_driver = NULL THEN
 RETURN ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_FS
 
 // Step 3: Read superblock
 superblock = fs_driver->read_super(device, flags)
 IF superblock = NULL THEN
 RETURN ERROR_MOUNT_FAILED
 
 // Step 4: Create mount structure
 mount = allocate_mount_entry()
 mount->superblock = superblock
 mount->root_inode = superblock->root_inode
 mount->mount_point = mount_point
 mount->fs_type = fs_driver
 
 // Step 5: Add to mount table
 add_to_mount_table(mount)
 update_mount_point_inode(mount_point, mount->root_inode)
 
 RETURN SUCCESS
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the kernel-level mount operation: validation, loading the appropriate file system driver, reading the superblock (metadata), creating a mount structure, and linking it to the global mount table. The VFS layer abstracts these details for different file systems.
